---
title: "Figures"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Set seed and source files 
```{r}
set.seed(1453)

source('Brownian_motion.R',keep.source = TRUE) # functions for Brownian motion simulations
source('EM.R',keep.source = TRUE) # functions for EM-simulation
source('plots.R',keep.source = TRUE) # functions for plotting
source('Strang.R',keep.source = TRUE) # functions for Strang splitting and likelihood
sourceCpp("fitzhugh_rcpp.cpp") # functions for Strang splitting and likelihood executed in C++ when speed is needed 
```

Configurations for FitzHugh-Nagumo simulation
```{r}
# Step configuration for EM simulation
N <- 300000
h <- 0.0001
T <- N*h
times <- seq(0,N*h,length.out=N+1)

# Configuration for sub-sampled paths
sub_h <- 0.06
ratio <- sub_h / h
sub_times <- seq(t_0,N*h,length.out=N/ratio+1)
idx <- seq(1,N+1,by=ratio)

# drift 
F <- function(x,theta = c(0.1,0.5,1.5,1.4)){
  # parameters
  eps <- theta[1]; alpha <- theta[2]; gamma <- theta[3]; beta <- theta[4]
  # variables
  X <- x[1]; Y <- x[2]
  c(
    (X-X^3-Y+alpha)/eps,
    gamma*X-Y+beta
  )
}

# additive noise
G <- function(x, Sigma = c(0.3,0.5)) Sigma
```


1000 FitzHugh-nagumo trajectories are stored in 'fitzhugh_paths.csv' to save computer time. Change sim_new_paths to TRUE to simulate new paths. 
```{r}
sim_new_paths <- FALSE

if (sim_new_paths){
  paths <- EM_sim_fast(F,G,x0=c(0,0),times,n_paths=1000,progress=TRUE)
  sub_paths <- lapply(paths, function(path) path[idx,])
  
  #write.csv(bind_rows(sub_paths, .id = "path_id"), "fitzhugh_paths.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}else{
  paths_df <- read.csv("fitzhugh_paths.csv")
  paths <- split(paths_df, paths_df$path_id)
}
```


Plots for Figure 1
```{r}
example_path <- EM_sim_fast(F,G,x0=c(0,0),times,n_paths=1,progress=FALSE)

# Plot one path
plot_2d_curve(example_path[[1]])

# Plot phase portrait of FHN drift
plot_FHN_phase_portrait(par=c(0.1,0.5,1.5,1.4))
```

Parameter estimation 
```{r}
# Tested methods (defined in fitzhugh_rcpp.cpp)
splitting_methods <- list(
    c('fix'),
    c('piecewise')
)

# method names (for overlay in plot)
splitting_labels <- c('Fixed point linearization','Piecewise Linearization')

# True parameters (for overlay in plot)
true_params <- c(0.1, 0.5, 1.5, 1.4, 0.3, 0.5)

start_params <- c(0.5, 0.1, 1, 1, 0.1, 0.1)
param_labels <- c("epsilon","alpha","gamma","beta","sigma[1]","sigma[2]")


fit_for_center <- function(center_vec, show_progress = FALSE) {
  n_paths <- length(paths)
  preds <- vector("list", n_paths)

  for (i in seq_along(paths)) {
    if (show_progress) message("  fitting path ", i, " / ", n_paths)
    this_path <- sub_paths[[i]]
    
    # objective: minimize negative log-likelihood
    obj <- function(par, path, times) {
      theta_drift     <- par[1:4]
      theta_diffusion <- par[5:6]
      
      # return NA if error
      ll <- tryCatch(
        log_lik_path_rcpp(as.matrix(path), times, theta_drift, theta_diffusion,
                            center = center_vec),
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
      
      # if ll is not a numeric value, return a big penalty
      if (!is.numeric(ll) || !is.finite(ll)) {
        return(1e20)   
      }
      return(ll)
    }
    
    fit <- optim(par    = start_params,
                 fn     = obj,
                 path   = this_path,
                 times  = sub_times,
                 method = "Nelder-Mead",
                 control = list(maxit = 2000,trace = 0)
                 )
    preds[[i]] <- fit$par
  }
  
  preds_mat <- do.call(rbind, preds)
  colnames(preds_mat) <- paste0("p", seq_along(start_params))
  out_df <- as.data.frame(preds_mat)
  
}

# Loop through different splitting methods
all_results_list <- vector("list", length(splitting_methods))
for (i in seq_along(splitting_methods)) {
  name <- splitting_labels[i]
  meth  <- splitting_methods[[i]]
  message("Running fits for method: ", cname)
  res_df <- fit_for_center(meth, show_progress = TRUE)

  res_df <- res_df %>% mutate(run = seq_len(nrow(res_df)), center = name)
  all_results_list[[i]] <- res_df
}
all_results <- bind_rows(all_results_list)

# Remove rows where all parameter estimates are NA (failed fits)
all_results <- all_results %>% filter(!apply(select(., starts_with("p")), 1, function(r) all(is.na(r))))

# format estimates for plotting
est_df <- all_results %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("p"), names_to = "param", values_to = "estimate") %>%
  mutate(param = factor(param, levels = paste0("p", seq_along(start_params)),
                        labels = param_labels),
         center = factor(center, levels = splitting_labels))

plot_estimates(est_df,true_params,param_labels,subtitle="1000 Monte Carlo repetitions")
```
